title: Playtest defaults file (playtest.yaml)
description: Shared defaults for every case at or below this directory; the nearest playtest.yaml wins per key. Case-only keys (story, tags, success, report) are config errors here — put them in the case file; a persona *list* (the fan-out form) is case-only too, but a scalar persona default is fine. max_steps/timeout may also be spelled nested under limits.

persona.description: Default persona the actor role-plays: built-in "tester" or "exploratory", or the name/slug of a personas/*.yaml file. Default "tester". Scalar only here (a suite-wide default actor); the persona *list* that fans a discovery study out is case-only — set it on the case.

mode.description: Case kind for every case below, default "journey". journey: regression test — deterministic success gate, baseline record/act/heal, pass/fail exit codes. discovery: product-insight study — no success gate, always a fresh agentic run that ends "explored" (give_up is a valid, informative outcome), graded with the discoverability rubric. A study directory sets mode: discovery here once.

vision.description: Discovery capability: send each step's viewport screenshot to the actor alongside the a11y snapshot and ask for visual observations (the step's "visual" field). Defaults to true when a case's resolved mode is discovery, false otherwise. "vision: true" resolving on a journey case is a config error — the regression instrument stays a11y-only by construction; "vision: false" is always allowed.

visual_regression.description: Internal: enable the pixel drift channel — hash each step's screenshot and compare on replay (a divergence escalates to heal, like a11y drift). No images are sent to the model. web only. Default true.

visual_regression_drift.description: Internal tuning knob for visual_regression: the Hamming-distance threshold (0-64) over the per-step screenshot dHash above which the screenshot reads as pixel drift. Higher = more tolerant (fewer drift heals); lower = stricter. Default 10.

lcp_ms.description: Threshold on the WORST navigation LCP in milliseconds: "< 2500" style (ops < <= > >=) or a bare number meaning <= n.

input_to_paint_ms.description: Threshold on the WORST input-to-paint latency in milliseconds; same format as lcp_ms.

perf.description: Latency thresholds (LCP / input-to-paint) checked with the journey gate. Silently irrelevant in discovery (the gate never runs). No-console-errors is a success criterion, not a perf key.

max_steps.description: Maximum actor steps before a run ends with end_reason max_steps. Mode-aware default when unset: 50 for a journey, 300 for a discovery run (which explores freely). Equivalent nested spelling: limits.max_steps.

timeout.description: Wall-clock limit per run: "5m", "90s", "250ms", or a number of milliseconds (must be > 0). Mode-aware default when unset: "4m" for a journey, "30m" for a discovery run. Equivalent nested spelling: limits.timeout.

limits.description: Nested spelling of max_steps/timeout; normalized to the top-level keys at load time.

parallel.description: Run-wide concurrency: how many cases run at once. An integer n bounds the pool to n; true uses the default pool (min(4, cores)). Or an object { total, record } for a single pool of `total` workers (int or true) where at most `record` of them may be RECORDING (driving the actor with the model) at any instant — cheap baseline checks fill the rest, so you can record at low concurrency while checking at high. `record` omitted means no cap. Omit `parallel` entirely to auto-select — serial for external apps (they may share mutable state), the default pool for managed (compose) selections. A run-wide knob, so set it in the top-level playtest.yaml; the CLI --parallel / --parallel-record flags override it.

actor_model.description: Model that role-plays the user. A short tier enum — "sonnet", "haiku", "opus", "gpt5_4_mini" (default), "gpt5_4", "gpt5_5" — resolves to a fully-qualified gateway model name (override per enum with PLAYTEST_<ENUM>_MODEL, e.g. PLAYTEST_SONNET_MODEL); any other value is used verbatim as an already-qualified name. A capable actor reads the page and recovers from friction far more reliably than a cheap one, and for both journeys and discovery the trajectory is only worth grading if the user was played well; pin a stronger tier ("sonnet"/"opus") when fidelity matters more than cost.

grader_model.description: Model that grades finished runs and checks assert: criteria. A short tier enum — "sonnet", "haiku", "opus", "gpt5_4_mini", "gpt5_4", or "gpt5_5" (default) — resolves to a fully-qualified gateway model name (override with PLAYTEST_<ENUM>_MODEL); any other value is used verbatim.

driver.description: Transport for the app under test. Default "web" (Playwright/Chromium). "mobile" drives a native iOS/Android app via Appium; "api" drives a REST API via fetch. A study/suite usually sets it once here. The other app.* keys, success kinds, and perf keys are validated per-driver.

base_url.description: Where the app under test runs, e.g. http://localhost:4173. Required for web and api (or pass --base-url); optional for mobile. Agents really click buy/delete/submit — point at staging or test, never production.

compose.description: docker-compose file path, relative to this file: managed mode — the harness boots and tears down the app per run. web/api.

init.description: Executable script path, relative to this file, run before each case to reset app state. Receives BASE_URL/RUN_ID. Any driver — also the mobile/api pre-auth path.

storage_state.description: Playwright storage-state JSON path, relative to this file, for a pre-authenticated browser session. web only.

platform.description: mobile only: the target platform (Appium XCUITest for ios, UiAutomator2 for android).

app.description: mobile only: path (relative to this file) to the app binary to install — a .app/.ipa (ios) or .apk (android). Required for the mobile driver.

device.description: mobile only: the target device/emulator/simulator name or udid. Omit to let the driver pick a default available device.

appium_url.description: mobile only: URL of a running Appium server, e.g. http://localhost:4723. Omit to use the default local Appium endpoint.

preserve_session.description: mobile only: keep the app installed and its data intact across runs instead of the default reinstall-and-wipe (maps to Appium's noReset). Off by default — every session reinstalls the binary and clears state, so the actor starts from a clean install each run. Set true when reinstalling is costly or would drop a login you've already established; the app is left installed with its data, so the actor stays authenticated between runs. The app's binary is still required and is installed on first launch.

openapi.description: api only: path (relative to this file) to an OpenAPI spec whose operations become the interactable "elements" the actor sees. Recommended for journey api cases.

width.description: Browser viewport width in CSS pixels. Default 1280.

height.description: Browser viewport height in CSS pixels. Default 720. A number captures VIEWPORT-ONLY stills (exactly what the user saw on screen — content below the fold is cut off, faithful to perception). Set null to capture FULL-PAGE stills (the whole scrollable page; good for debugging scroll or marketing shots). Whichever is chosen drives BOTH the on-disk PNG and the vision image sent to the actor.

viewport.description: web only: the Chromium viewport in CSS pixels, e.g. { width: 1366, height: 768 } for a smaller desktop. Default { width: 1280, height: 720 }. Shapes the layout the app renders at; the height also decides whether stills are viewport-only (a number) or full-page (null). Riding manifest.pins.viewport, this is part of the comparability key — runs with a different viewport (including viewport-only vs full-page) are not compared.

device_scale_factor.description: web only: the Chromium device scale factor (DPI multiplier) for step screenshots. Default 1; set 2 for retina-crisp, higher-resolution stills (the on-disk PNG renders at 2x the pixel density). Purely a rendering-quality knob — it does NOT ride manifest.pins, so it is not part of the comparability key and a run at a different scale factor still compares against the baseline. The vision image sent to the actor is still capped at the longest-edge limit, so raising this sharpens the disk artifact without changing token cost.

dom_quiet_ms.description: web only: how long the DOM must go without mutations before the page counts as settled. Default 500.

net_quiet_ms.description: web only: how long there must be no in-flight network requests before the page counts as settled. Default 500.

source_quiet_ms.description: mobile only: how long the accessibility tree must go unchanged before the screen counts as settled. Default 400.

max_ms.description: Hard ceiling on the settle wait — a screen that never goes quiet (e.g. a poller) stops waiting here at max_ms. Default 2000.

settle.description: web + mobile: override the driver's settle quiet windows. After every action the driver waits until things go quiet (web: DOM AND network for dom_quiet_ms/net_quiet_ms; mobile: the accessibility tree for source_quiet_ms) — capped at max_ms — before snapshotting, so the actor always sees a stable page. Lower the windows for a fast app to shave the per-step floor; raise them for an async-heavy one. The FIRST settle of a session (initial load) waits longer — initial_quiet_ms, default max(2× the per-step window, 500ms) — so a lagging app doesn't settle on a blank first page. Use the keys for your driver (web: dom_quiet_ms/net_quiet_ms; mobile: source_quiet_ms; both: max_ms/initial_quiet_ms). Riding manifest.pins.settle, this is part of the comparability key — a tuned value means runs with a different value are not compared.

cookies.description: web only: cookies to set on the browser context before the first navigation, as a flat { name: value } map, e.g. { bvt: "true" }. Scoped domain-wide (base_url's host, path "/") so they ride EVERY request the app makes — useful for routing to a blue/green slot or a feature flag. A session input (like storage_state), not a manifest pin: a run with different cookies still compares against the baseline.

base_url.description: This environment's URL, e.g. https://stg.example.com. The main thing that varies per env.

cookies.description: Per-env cookies ({ name: value }) — e.g. a blue/green-slot or feature-flag cookie that only staging needs. Replaces the top-level app.cookies wholesale (no merge).

storage_state.description: Path (relative to this file) to a Playwright storage-state JSON — a saved cookies + localStorage snapshot of an already-logged-in browser session, so the actor starts authenticated instead of signing in. Per-env because each environment usually has its own accounts/tokens (a dev login won't authenticate against staging). web only.

init.description: Path (relative to this file) to a per-env state-reset/seed script run before each case (receives BASE_URL/RUN_ID) — use when an environment needs a different seed than the default.

envs.description: Named environment overlays selected with --env <name>. Each <name> is a partial app.* block (base_url/cookies/storage_state/init) whose keys SHALLOW-override the top-level app.* (an env inherits the defaults, then replaces per key — e.g. envs.stg.cookies replaces the top-level cookies wholesale, no deep merge). Use for the same story across dev/staging/prod: { t2: { base_url: ... }, stg: { base_url: ..., cookies: { BVT: "true" } } }. With no --env the top-level app.* runs; --base-url still wins over --env. Transport-level keys (driver/compose/mobile/api config) are deliberately NOT per-env, and envs cannot nest.

app.description: App under test. Merges per-key down the defaults chain, so deeper files or the case can override just base_url. The app.driver key selects which subset applies; a key set for the wrong driver is a config error. app.envs declares named environment overlays selected at run time with --env <name>.
